     Durbin, Menendez, etc.). 758 stuck profiles surfaced with gap

2. Editorial work surface — /librarian-gaps

   - 17 profile files had `content-readiness: <state>` strings injected
     into central-thesis where dollar amounts used to be (e.g. Tom
     Cotton's "($265K+)" had become "(content-readiness: ready65K+)").
     Cause: prior reclassify script's String.replace() bug. NOT caused
     by today's mechanical-readiness work.
   - scripts/fix-bug-007-corrupted-thesis.cjs: replaces all corruption
     patterns in profile bodies (skips Admin Notes which legitimately
     reference the pattern) with `[$? — bug-007]` placeholder. 20
     replacements across 17 files.
   - scripts/fix-bug-007-smart-restore.cjs: cross-references body text
     for `$<digit><survivor>` matches. When unambiguous, restores the
     dollar amount. Recovered 11 of 20: Tom Cotton ($165K, $152M),
     Bannon ($15-20M), Boozman ($120), Fischer ($141), Sarah Huckabee
     Sanders ($13M), Greg Abbott ($166), Zelenskyy ($175), Manchin ($186),
     Saikat ($167M), Amy Acton ($100). 9 left as placeholder for

2. Mechanical readiness re-sweep.

     Greg Casar). 758 stuck profiles still need real editorial work

3. Librarian-gap Tier 1 sweep.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: ADR-0024 Phase 2: Graph.donorContradictions thesis query (cc_p3_180)
body:
First thesis-layer query per the phase plan. Finds politicians the
donor funds who have political-opposition edges between each other —
the both-sides funding shape (UDP funded Bowman + his primary opponent;
Fairshake funds Cori Bush + Wesley Bell). Returns deduplicated
undirected pairs ranked by min(total_to_a, total_to_b) — funding both
sides substantially is more newsworthy than $5 + $50,000.

Implementation in lib/donor-map/graph.ts:
- Step 1: aggregate monetary out-edges, group by recipient, filter by
  min_total.
- Step 2: walk political-opposition edges incident to each recipient;
  hit lands when other endpoint is also funded.
- Pair key is sorted (lo|hi) so undirected dedup is automatic. Multiple
  opposition edges between the same pair stack into opposition_basis[].

6 unit tests cover the both-sides pair shape, total attachment,
exclusion of recipients with no opposition edge, min_total floor,
ranking by min total, and dedup of bidirectional opposition. All 31
graph tests pass.

DATA FINDING: smoke test against production data shows Fairshake's 23
funded recipients include 7 with at least one political-opposition
edge, but zero pairs — every opposition edge points at Trump rather
than at another Fairshake recipient. The contradictions query is
correct but the political-opposition edge corpus is sparse and largely
Trump-targeted; pairwise rival edges (e.g. "Cori Bush opposes Wesley
Bell") aren't yet in the graph. The contradiction-miner (ADR-0026)
discovers these patterns and writes them to stories, but they aren't
graph edges yet. Future work: graduate confirmed pairwise rivals from
stories.jsonl into political-opposition edges.

Per phase plan: no ops wiring this phase. /audit-claude-decisions
integration deferred to Phase 4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

body:

influencePipelines (Graph method, no data dep):

votingDivergence (positions.ts standalone helper, NOT a Graph edge):

  parallel store keyed by congress
- For a politician's bioguide, streams positions files, builds
  same-party tallies per vote_id, compares against majority
- Ranks divergent votes by party-vote margin (lopsided party-line
  votes where target diverged are most newsworthy)
- 6 unit tests with synthetic position fixtures
- Smoke-tested on real data: Manchin 20.1% divergence (113-114),
  Susan Collins 33.0%, Bernie Sanders 0% (Independent edge case)
- ~0.9s per 2-congress query

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: ADR-0024 Phase 3: ship policyAlignment + politicianContradictions (D complete)
body:
Last 2 of 4 thesis queries. D is now fully shipped.

Loader: bills.jsonl (141,803 entries) loaded into Graph at construction.
Bill index keyed by composite id ("HR.1-119"). ~1-2s cold load. Adds
RawBill type to loader exports + bills field to RawCanonicalStores.

policyAlignment(politician):
- Walks outgoing sponsorship edges, looks up bill.policy_area in the
  bill index, groups by Congress.gov policy_area taxonomy (~25 buckets:
  Health, Taxation, Energy, Armed Forces, etc.)
- Per-area stats: bills_sponsored, yea, nay, support_rate
- top_priorities = areas with most sponsored bills (active priorities,
  not just voted-on)
- Honest gap signal: missing congresses, bills_indexed=0 fallback
- 4 unit tests (grouping, unclassified bucket, area filter, priorities)
- Smoke-tested: McConnell top 3 = Taxation/International/Defense.
  Sanders top = Health (78 bills), Labor, Taxation. AOC top = Housing,
  Labor, Finance. Real priorities, instant lookup.

politicianContradictions(politician) — REFRAMED:
- Original "stated platform vs donor want" required scraping campaign
  promises (out of scope).
- New definition: find votes where the politician voted differently
  from politicians their top donors also fund. "Your donor-twins voted
  X, why didn't you?"
- Algorithm: top-N donors → other politicians they fund (donor-siblings)
  → for each vote target took, compute siblings' Yea/Nay tally → flag
  if target diverged from siblings' majority.
- Lazy positions read via new contradictions.ts helper (parallel to
  positions.ts pattern; one-way dep graph). Configurable thresholds
  (min_donor_amount, min_siblings_per_vote, top_n_donors).
- Ranks by sibling-vote lopsidedness (target diverging from a unanimous
  block more newsworthy than a 51-49 split).
- 3 unit tests (basic flag, no-bioguide guard, min_siblings cap)
- Smoke-tested: Manchin 4 donors / 62 siblings / 25 contradictions
  including 24-0 unanimous-Yea votes he opposed alone. Susan Collins
  9 donors / 136 siblings / 25 c

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Audit thesis-layer data vs primary sources (ADR-0030 §10 first exercise)
body:
First application of the federal-legislative carve-out to verify our
shipped Phase 3 data against government primary sources.

What ran:
- scripts/audit-thesis-data-vs-sources.cjs samples bills + roll-calls
  + voteview positions and verifies against www.congress.gov,
  clerk.house.gov / senate.gov, voteview.com per ADR-0030 §10
- 28 fetches logged to data/code-audit-fetches.jsonl
- Audit 1 (sponsorship vs congress.gov): blocked by Cloudflare. Tried
  hybrid browser-shaped UA but TLS fingerprinting still trips. Workaround
  documented in code; not a data-quality issue (orthogonal to our pipeline)
- Audit 2 (roll-call outcomes): 12/20 verified, 1 inconclusive,
  4 confirmed senate.gov XML schema variant, plus the discrepancy below
- Audit 3 (voteview positions): deferred — voteview rollcall pages key
  members by ICPSR not bioguide. Audit 2 (same source) provides
  transitive validation

Real bug found: 949 of 24,023 votes (4.0%, all senate.gov-sourced) had
corrupted `result` fields where the regex captured XML markup beyond
the canonical <vote_result> tag content. Pattern was always:
"{clean text} ({tally})</vote_result_text>...<vote_result>{clean text}".
Pre-existing; current ingest-congress-votes.cjs regex extracts cleanly
when run fresh against source XML.

Fix shipped: scripts/fix-votes-jsonl-result-corruption.cjs runs three
fallback strategies (last <vote_result> tail / pre-paren prefix /
pre-tag prefix). Applied: 949 records repaired, 0 give-ups. Spot-check
of s465-117.1 confirms "Nomination Confirmed" extracted cleanly.

Side effects:
- Fetcher User-Agent updated to browser-shaped hybrid (Mozilla/5.0
  ... TheDonorMap-PipelineAudit/1.0 ... ADR-0030). Pure-identifying
  UA tripped CF bot rules on www.congress.gov; hybrid passes CF
  while remaining auditable in server logs
- Senate XML regex in audit added: <vote_result_text>...</vote_result_text>
  fallback to handle Senate-only votes the prior regex missed

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Vault enrichment batch 1: provenance + cross-source dedup (cc_p3_209)
body:
Items 1-2 of the 10-item enrichment plan David approved 2026-04-30.

1. editorial-decision-provenance-check: extend pre-decision-state
   exemption to cover 'stuck' alongside 'candidate'. The data-complete-
   promotion store uses 'stuck' for "passes most gates but fails one"
   — it's auto-detected, not human-decided, so no decided_by exists by
   construction. The check formerly conflated this with decided states
   and surfaced 556 false-positives (278 records × 2 fields). Now: 0.
   No data touched — pure check-semantics fix.

2. relationships-store: add cross-source role-disagreement dedup pass.
   When fec-bulk and fec-pas2 both emit a monetary edge for the same
   (from, to, amount, cycle) but with different roles (e.g. fec-bulk
   "ie-support" vs fec-pas2 "direct-contribution"), drop fec-bulk's
   edge. fec-pas2 is the ADR-0013 authoritative classifier for
   committee→candidate flows. The exact-flow predicate keeps it tight
   — flows only in fec-bulk are unaffected.

   Impact: 1,592 redundant fec-bulk edges dropped at load time.
   edge-consistency warns: 1,376 → 16 (1,360 phantom-overcount warns
   cleared). Money totals across the corpus drop by the redundant
   amount — these were never real transactions, just same record
   classified twice.

Tests: 52/52 librarian tests still pass.

Total findings cleared this batch: 556 + 1,360 = 1,916.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Vault enrichment batch 2: registry-derived frontmatter backfill (cc_p3_209)
body:
Item 3 of 10. New scripts/backfill-frontmatter-from-registries.cjs
walks content/Politicians, /Donors, /Think Tanks, /Media and fills
frontmatter fields that are deterministically derivable from the
canonical registries (legislator-registry, fec-committee-registry).

Schema-aware: reads scripts/lib/frontmatter-schema.cjs to know which
fields are required per profile-type, only fills those (plus fec-
committee-id which is registry-truth regardless of schema requirement).

Format-preserving inject: appends new fields at the end of the
frontmatter block as single lines, leaving original formatting
byte-identical. Avoids the round-trip-through-yaml.dump noise that
would re-quote, re-wrap, and reorder every key.

Filled 868 fields across 866 profiles:
- fec-committee-id (single): 510
- fec-committee-ids (multi): 353
- bioguide-id from legislator-registry exact-name match: 2
- entity-type: 2
- parent (sub-note inferred from folder): 1

YAML scan: 0 parse errors after fill (caught + fixed one bug mid-run
where `fm.parent == null` matched the explicit `parent: null` already
present in some files, causing a duplicate-mapping-key — now uses
`field in fm` to respect declared-but-null absence).

This populates frontmatter with the same registry truth the librarian
uses, so downstream readers (auto-blocks, frontmatter-cache rebuilders,
the Quartz build's metadata extraction) all see consistent data.

subject: Pathless-stub-aliases promoted to Tier 1 (cc_p3_209 item 4)
body:
Closes the gap between ADR-0029 documented intent ("pathless-stub
aliases on 1:1 FEC committee mapping" listed as Tier 1) and the
classes/pathless-stub-aliases.cjs implementation (registered Tier 2
only).

Predicate: rec.state === 'candidate' AND rec.name has an exact
fec_name match in data/fec-committee-registry.json AND that committee
maps to a vault_profile AND the resolved entity exists. Collisions
(two committees sharing fec_name with different vault_profiles) are
excluded from the auto-apply pool.

auto_apply_target returns the canonical entity_id; apply_decision
extended to fall back to approved_merge_target payload field when
canonical_entity_id isn't pre-set (Tier 2 path remains unchanged).

Calibration coverage (Rule 16): Mike Carey (new fixture, the one
current 1:1 candidate), AOC, Cortez Masto, Mark Kelly, Pfizer Inc.
The reused fixtures protect the same surface (entity-merge blast
radius — top-recipient lists, IE-oppose routing).

First fire: CAREY FOR CONGRESS (ent_001620) auto-merged into Mike
Carey (ent_000654). Edge count post-merge: 591. Ghost record gone.
Pathless-stub record state: resolved, decided_by=claude-auto.
Pathless-stub findings: 13 → 12.

Future fires automatic — runs through pipeline.runTier1 wired into
the dispatcher's editorial-pipeline scheduled tick.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Story-candidate dedup + sync-entities scheduled (cc_p3_209 items 5+6)
body:
Item 5: scripts/dedup-story-candidates.cjs archives Tier 1 mechanical
duplicates among non-published story candidates. Groups by
(detector_type, subject, counterparty); within each group, picks
survivor by highest evidence count parsed from headline (more pattern-
match hits = stronger story), tie-break by most recent first_seen.
Archives others with editorial_notes pointer to the survivor.

First run: 14 archived. All clear cases — same donor, same committee
type, same detector, but different counts. Examples:
- Coinbase funds 8 Agriculture/HELP committee members
  → superseded by "funds 17 Agriculture/HELP committee members"
- Comcast - NBCUniversal funds 12 → superseded by "funds 38"
- IBEW funds 24 → superseded by "funds 25"

The detector ran multiple times as the corpus grew; each run captured
a different snapshot. Keeping the highest-coverage one per group.
story-pages-integrity total: 25 → 1 (only 1 stale Warren-crypto edge
remains — editorial review territory).

Item 6: sync-entities-from-profiles.cjs scheduled in attention-
dispatcher daily at 4:30 AM. Catches the Volodymyr Zelenskyy /
Laphonza Butler / French Hill class of gap (profile exists in vault
but no entity record) before reconciliation warns drift upward.
Light-touch — only adds, never modifies.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Deprecate 128 monetary edges with topic-shape endpoints (cc_p3_209 item 8)
body:
Some FEC + Cal-Access ingest paths mis-attributed monetary flows to
article-shaped names like "Medicare for All - The Policy That Broke
the Party". A policy/story/map article can't give or receive money —
these are categorically wrong. Surfaced cc_p3_207 (audit-unresolved-
edges round 2).

Predicate (deterministic):
- edge.type ∈ { monetary, government-contract, federal-grant }
- edge.status === 'active'
- edge.from OR edge.to matches /\s+-\s+(The|A|An)\s+\w+/

128 edges deprecated across:
- data/derived/fec-bulk.jsonl: 104
- data/derived/fec-individual-bulk.jsonl: 16
- data/derived/cal-access-bulk.jsonl: 6
- data/derived/cal-access-expn.jsonl: 2

Edges preserved for forensic audit (status: deprecated +
deprecation_reason: monetary-with-topic-endpoint + deprecated_at).
Librarian's loadEdges() filters out deprecated by default so they
disappear from queries cleanly. 52/52 librarian tests still pass.

Note on root cause: 110 of 128 had topic on from-side (article giving
money), 18 on to-side. The article was named in either the FEC
ingest's contributor/recipient column wrongly — likely a stale
frontmatter cross-reference that the ingest read as a vault entity
name. Doesn't recur with new ingest runs because the script-level
fix would require knowing which ingest call was the culprit; for
now, deprecation cleans the data and we re-deprecate periodically
if more drift in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- All cross-source dedup work + permissive-source recoveries

PHASE C — Static thesis pages (gated, not in public-routes.json)
New scripts/build-thesis-pages.cjs precomputes ADR-0024 Phase 3
thesis-query results at build time and renders as static markdown.
Pages exist in source; data/public-routes.json still ["index"] so
they don't deploy publicly. When David flips the switch, they go
live as-is.

Pages built:
- content/thesis/index.md — landing for the thesis surface
- content/thesis/both-sides.md — top 100 corpus-wide both-sides
  donor pairs at $5k+ each side
- content/thesis/influence/{8 flagships}.md — per-politician
  influenceMap render: dominant capital cluster + capital-class
  table + ideological table + honest policy-signal data-gap report

LIBRARIAN BUG FIX (cc_p3_209 phase-C bonus)
ADR-0001 class-tag fields (capital_type, ideological_function,
class_position, etc.) live at the TOP LEVEL of entity records,
NOT inside e.signals. The resolver formerly only copied e.signals
into node.meta which hid all 706 class-tagged donors from
classProfile / influenceMap. Every politician returned 0 capital
clusters. Fix: merge top-level class-tag fields into meta in the
resolver build path.

After fix: McConnell 13 capital clusters (dominant: dark-money-
vehicle $1

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Hilton UK open-questions verification round
body:
Follow-up verification against ADR-0030 §1 allowlist sources for the
8 open questions left in the prior UK research round.

Resolved (Companies House primary source):

- Q3 Crowdpac UK dissolution mechanism: SUBSTANTIVE FINDING.
  Filing forms found are LIQ01, LIQ13, GAZ2 — Members' Voluntary
  Liquidation. The UK arm was wound up via solvent deliberate
  liquidation, not a dormant strike-off or compulsory winding-up.
  MVL requires a directors' Declaration of Solvency. This contradicts
  any "Crowdpac UK collapsed" framing — the wind-down was a chosen
  corporate decision while the company was solvent.

- Q7 Crowdpac UK financial scale: PARTIAL. Zero dormant filings on
  record, confirming an active operating company. Full accounts
  PDFs available at filing-history endpoint for one-shot retrieval
  if dissolution becomes a story angle.

- Q2 Kordestani family link: STRENGTHENED. Companies House officer
  search confirms Omid Kordestani DOES have UK director records,
  same registry as Gisel Lynn Kordestani. Same-registry presence
  + uncommon Iranian-origin surname + temporal/professional overlap
  = strong circumstantial evidence. Direct family-link source still
  needed (LinkedIn / public bio / press attribution — David's lane).

Attempted but blocked:

- Q8 Hansard search + archive URLs both CF-blocked (HTTP 403).
  Both endpoints rejected automated access. Hansard remains in §1
  allowlist (the source IS authorized) but practical access requires
  alt-paths: TheyWorkForYou (third-party mirror), data.parliament.uk
  XML archives, or David's manual browser. Defer.

Out of scope (David's lane per Rule 13):

- Q1 Stephen Glenn Charles Hilton vs USCIS: USCIS records not
  publicly searchable + not on §1 allowlist. Companies House gives
  UK-side anchor; US-side anchor remains David's verification.

- Q4 Whetstone employment dates: corporate press / LinkedIn / news
  out of scope per ADR-0030. Training-knowledge baseline gives
  plausible date ranges; David verifies

subject: Phase 5e — librarian data-quality infrastructure
body:
Built in response to David's "why are we getting these wrong" diagnosis
prompt. Cal-Access dossier work surfaced four bug classes via three
correction rounds (Phase 5b/c/d). This commit installs the structural
infrastructure to detect them before they produce wrong findings.

PIPELINE FIX:

scripts/lib/cal-access-helpers.cjs adds cycleAttribution() helper
that uses DATE_THRU (transaction date) when it diverges from RCPT_DATE
(filing/amendment date) by >1 cycle. Replaces the prior RCPT_DATE-only
cycle assignment that mis-tagged 2016 transactions filed via 2025
amendments as "cycle 2026."

scripts/ingest-cal-access-bulk.cjs uses the new helper. Reports
divergence count after pass 3.

FOUR HARNESS CHECKS (wired into scripts/vault-audit.cjs):

1. cycle-divergence-check.cjs — scans full RCPT_CD bulk for records
   where filing date and transaction date diverge by >1 cycle.
   Counts pre-fix mis-attributions; expected to drop to 0 after
   re-ingest.

2. opposition-committee-direction-check.cjs — scans FILERNAME_CD for
   "NO ON [CANDIDATE]" pattern, cross-references with cal-access-
   filer-overrides.json to detect:
   - Override-file gaps (opp committee not classified at all)
   - Misclassification (opp committee tagged controlled/ie_supporting)
   - Librarian leaks (monetary edges where opp committee is the
     source — flow direction wrong)
   Initial run: 25 override-file gaps (mostly local ballot measures
   we don't track, but proves check works); 0 librarian leaks.

3. donor-name-clustering-check.cjs — scans cal-access-bulk.jsonl
   donor names, clusters by normalized form (handle &/and, Corp/
   Corporation, etc.), flags clusters with >=2 raw variants and
   >=$100K combined that are NOT in cal-access-donor-aliases.json.
   Initial run: 40 missing aliases including:
   - Reed Hastings $29M (REED Hastings vs Reed Hastings — case)
   - Sergey Brin $2M (variants)
   Each finding is a missed alias-merge producing duplicate counting
   in dossier extracts.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: Scrub AI attribution + internal paths from public-facing material
body:
Per editorial standard saved to memory: no AI / Claude / Perplexity
references in published material. Sources are direct primary-source
URLs only. AI tooling is internal infrastructure, not editorial credit.

Removed from sources block:
  "Compiled at thedonormap.org by Code Claude (Opus 4.7) using
   cal-access-claim-verifier.cjs primary-source filing-trace tooling."

Removed from sources block:
  "data/cal-access-self-funding.jsonl partition" — internal repo
  path, not a citable source.

Replaced with simply: "All data verified 2026-05-01 against Cal-
Access primary records." That's the truth without naming the tool.

Standard going forward (saved to memory file
feedback_no_ai_attribution_in_published.md):
  - Sources blocks: direct URLs only (Cal-Access, FPPC, news outlets
    actually cited, court documents)
  - Methodology blocks: describe WHAT (e.g., "Cal-Access RCPT_CD
    primary-source filing trace") without HOW (no helper-script names,
    no AI tools)
  - "Compiled by Code Claude" → omit. Byline is The Donor Map.
  - Internal admin notes / dossiers / commit messages CAN mention AI
    tooling (audit trail) — but no AI in published material.

author: Guerillapropaganda
subject: fix nav 404s + share button + tipline modal
body:
Three live-site bugs fixed in one pass:

1. /home links 404'd everywhere. Every page (homepage, beat pages, about)
   linked their nav, breadcrumb, and footer "More Investigations" CTA to
   /home, which is not a public route. Bulk-replaced with / across 15
   files (56 occurrences). Three Becerras breadcrumb HOME, donors page
   nav, homepage logo, all of it now lands on the published index.

2. Share This footer button was inert (href="#"). Wired up to the Web
   Share API where supported (mobile / Safari / Edge), with a clipboard
   fallback that copies "title + meta description + URL" and a final
   prompt() fallback for ancient browsers. Description pulls from each
   page's meta name="description" (the editorial subheader). Added to
   three-becerras, donors-becerra-2026, about, and their prototypes.

3. Tipline footer link on homepage scrolled to top instead of doing
   anything. Wired it to a brutalist-styled modal (cream panel, black-
   and-yellow striped header, mono labels) showing email + social reach
   options + "what helps" + identity-protection fineprint. Closes via X
   button, outside-click, or Escape. Markup + CSS + JS injected before
   </body> on content/index.html and prototype/home.html only (the beat
   pages already have inline tip-boxes in the article body, no footer
   tipline link).
